global_section_rules.module_expansion_scope: Only expand feature-detail module prototypes under 4.0. Do not duplicate 1.0, 2.0, 3.0, or the 4.0 section header per module.

warning_red.purpose: AI待确认 label derived from the template label/tag style.
warning_red.contrast_rule: Use a red-series label background/fill with white text for AI pending labels; keep the label template-derived and readable at a glance.

project_title.policy: Fill when source exists; otherwise preserve placeholder. Text-only edit.

planner_owner.policy: Fill when source exists; otherwise preserve placeholder. Text-only edit.

ued_owner.policy: Fill when source exists; otherwise preserve placeholder. Text-only edit.

date.policy: Fill generation date unless explicitly preserved. Text-only edit.

overview.layout_policy: Always preserve asymmetric layout. Never render equal-width cards.

problem.fixed_question_text: 设计背景和要解决的问题

version_table.first_generation_rule: Keep table header plus one filled row.
version_table.iteration_rule: Append a new row below existing rows; do not overwrite history.

rules: 3.0 uses Goal Flow Graphs: each top-level graph is a player goal, phrased as player wants to..., not a linear step strip.
A screen node contains only interface/state name and a rectangular screen placeholder.
Flow node prototypes are style prototypes only; do not infer node count, order, single-line layout, or branch topology from them.
Designer Flow Grammar may add diamond decisions, branches, merges, loops, dashed optional routes, mode lanes, external-surface nodes, and nested sub-flow nodes when needed for readability.
Added flow grammar must serve Goal Flow Graph readability; do not use it as decoration or to turn backend mechanisms into top-level player flows.
Complex sub-processes may be folded into a nested sub-flow node only when input, output, and return path are clear.
Before drawing flow topology, create an Action / Affordance Inventory from the 4.0 surfaces; small entries, messages, jump links, and controls may create real branches.
Do not collapse distinct affordances on the same surface when they lead to different destinations, feedback, recovery, or loop closure.

style_only_policy.drawing_rule: Use template visual language, not template topology. Draw from the actual Goal Flow Graph; default reading direction is left-to-right and top-to-bottom, but branches, returns, lanes, external surfaces, and nested subflows may use new rows or groups for readability.

rules: Label/tag stays above the explanation group.
AI待确认 derives from the tag style and uses warning_red.

feature_detail.hierarchy_rule: major function = 主标题; minor function = 副标题; interface explanation = 界面标题 + left wireframe + right notes.

footer.policy: Move to board bottom when height expands; do not redraw.

forbidden: Do not edit source template node.
Do not hand-draw existing template chips, labels, version rows, dividers, or screen frames.
Do not change top floating bar style.
Do not fill 2.0 adaptation by default.
Do not render 1.0 as equal-width cards.
Do not fall back to freehand drawing when a slot is missing.
Do not infer flow node count, order, or layout from flow style prototypes.
Do not compress multiple player goals into one template-like horizontal flow path.
Do not use a 1334x750 screen frame as a state collection or mechanism summary.
Do not inherit template example topology: 7-node strips, single-line paths, fixed branch shape, and placeholder order are not content rules.
Do not pass AI pending QA by board-level presence count; coverage must follow player goals and high-risk decisions.
Do not expose internal taxonomy or template reasoning in visible right-side notes.
Do not omit visible affordance branches such as entry links, messages, red dots, building hotspots, mail items, or helper controls just to keep a template-like flow.
Do not treat one generic AI pending item as sufficient when multiple player goals or high-risk affordances remain unresolved.

department_label_component.resolution: inspect componentProperties or variantProperties at runtime; do not edit inner text
